title: Shortfall interest charge – supporting information
description: Information to provide with your objection about a shortfall interest charge.
keywords: shortfall, charge, interest,  shortfall interest charge

content_blocks.text: To lodge an objection about a shortfall interest charge:

items: complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
provide the supporting information listed below.

content_blocks.text: Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for an objection about a shortfall interest charge includes:

items: reasons why you disagree with our decision to apply or not to remit the shortfall interest charge

details of the circumstances that resulted in the tax shortfall period (the day the tax debt was due or would have been due for payment if the shortfall was reported correctly, and the day before the notice of assessment was issued which included the amount of tax shortfall)
details of the efforts you made to reduce or avoid the tax shortfall period
whether the reason for the tax shortfall period was directly attributable to a natural disaster, other disasters or serious illness
an explanation if you believe the ATO may have contributed to the delay
a chronological history of events leading to the tax shortfall period
any additional information that supports your claims.

content_text:
## How to lodge
To lodge an objection about a shortfall interest charge:
- complete and submit the relevant form Objection form – for taxpayers Objection form – for tax professionals
- provide the supporting information listed below.
## Supporting information
Providing this information up front will reduce delays.
If you've previously given us any of the information listed below, you don't need to resend it.
The information we need for an objection about a shortfall interest charge includes:
- reasons why you disagree with our decision to apply or not to remit the shortfall interest charge
- details of the circumstances that resulted in the tax shortfall period (the day the tax debt was due or would have been due for payment if the shortfall was reported correctly, and the day before the notice of assessment was issued which included the amount of tax shortfall)
- details of the efforts you made to reduce or avoid the tax shortfall period
- whether the reason for the tax shortfall period was directly attributable to a natural disaster, other disasters or serious illness
- an explanation if you believe the ATO may have contributed to the delay
- a chronological history of events leading to the tax shortfall period
- any additional information that supports your claims.
